for

![][image1]

by

ADAM BIN ARBAIN

![Hubungi kami - ehilal][image2]

1. # Introduction {#introduction}

   1. ## Purpose {#purpose}

The purpose of this document is to describe the user interface (UI) design of the AquaLeaf system. This specification outlines the layout, functionality, and interaction flow of the graphical user interfaces used in the AquaLeaf mobile application and web portal. The document provides visual references and descriptions of each interface screen to illustrate how users interact with the system when performing tasks such as seaweed quality scanning, report generation, and administrative management.

This document serves as a reference for understanding the interface structure of the AquaLeaf system and documents the visual and functional design of the application interfaces.

2. ## Scope {#scope}

This document covers the graphical user interfaces implemented in the AquaLeaf system, including both the mobile application used for seaweed quality assessment and the web-based platform used for farm management and administrative operations. It describes the primary interface screens, their visual components, and the user interactions associated with each interface.

The scope of this UI specification includes:

* Mobile application interfaces used by seaweed farmers for login, seaweed species selection, seaweed scanning, and viewing scan results.  
* Web portal interfaces used by farm managers to register farm accounts, access dashboards, and generate seaweed quality reports.  
* Administrative web interfaces used by system administrators to monitor system usage, review system event logs, and manage registered farm accounts.

2. # User Interface Overview {#user-interface-overview}

   1. ## Mobile Application Interface {#mobile-application-interface}

The AquaLeaf mobile application is designed to assist seaweed farmers in conducting on-site seaweed quality assessments. The interface prioritizes simplicity and ease of use to accommodate farmers with minimal technical experience. The mobile interface includes features for account login, seaweed species selection, seaweed scanning, and viewing quality analysis results.

2. ## Web Portal Interface {#web-portal-interface}

The AquaLeaf web portal provides management and monitoring functionality for farm managers and system administrators. Farm managers can generate quality reports and monitor scan data, while administrators can manage farm accounts, view system logs, and monitor system usage statistics.

3. ## UI Design Principles {#ui-design-principles}

* The interface uses **large buttons and simple layouts** to ensure usability in outdoor farming environments.  
* Camera scanning features are designed for **minimal user interaction** to allow quick seaweed quality checks.  
* Web dashboards present information using **tables and summary statistics** for efficient monitoring.

3. # Mobile Application UI Screens {#mobile-application-ui-screens}

   1. ## Login Screen {#login-screen}

The Login screen allows users to authenticate into the AquaLeaf mobile application using their registered farm account credentials. Successful authentication grants access to the seaweed scanning and quality monitoring features of the application.

***![][image3]![][image4]***  
*Figure 3.1.1: AquaLeaf Login Screen*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Email Input Field | Allows user to enter the registered email address |
| Password Input Field | Allows user to enter account password |
| Login Button | Submits credentials for authentication |
| Loading Indicator | Displays authentication process status |

2. ## Seaweed Scanning Guide {#seaweed-scanning-guide}

The Seaweed Scanning Guide is displayed during the first login to provide instructions on how to properly perform seaweed quality scanning using the mobile application. The guide consists of multiple pages explaining the scanning interface and recommended camera positioning.

***![][image5]![][image6]![][image7]***  
*Figure 3.2.1: Seaweed Scanning Guide Page 1, 2, and 3*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Instruction Panel | Displays guidance text and visual instructions |
| Navigation Controls | Allows users to navigate between guide pages |
| Done Button | Completes onboarding and proceeds to species selection |

3. ## Set Seaweed Species Screen {#set-seaweed-species-screen}

This screen allows the user to select the seaweed species that will be analyzed during scanning. The selected species determines the AI model used for quality detection.

![][image8]  
*Figure 3.3.1: Select Seaweed Species Screen*

![][image9]  
*Figure 3.3.2: Species Information Screen*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Species Selection Buttons | Allows user to select Red, Green, or Brown seaweed |
| Information Icon | Displays additional information about each species |

4. ## Set Seaweed Type Screen {#set-seaweed-type-screen}

This screen allows the user to select the type of seaweed to be analyzed. The selected type influences the interpretation of quality results returned by the AI model.

![][image10]  
*Figure 3.4.1: Select Seaweed Type Screen*

![][image11]  
*Figure 3.4.2: Type Information Screen*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Type Selection Buttons | Allows selection between Fresh or Dried seaweed |
| Information Icon | Displays additional information about each type |

5. ## Home Screen {#home-screen}

The Home screen serves as the main control interface of the AquaLeaf mobile application. From this screen, users can configure scanning parameters, view synchronization status, and access the seaweed scanning feature.

***![][image12]***  
*Figure 3.5.1: Home Screen*

![][image13]  
*Figure 3.5.2: Impurity Threshold Settings*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Farm Information Panel | Displays farm account details |
| Last Sync Indicator | Displays time of last successful scan synchronization |
| Set Seaweed Species Button | Allows user to change species selection |
| Set Seaweed Type Button | Allows user to change seaweed type |
| Impurity Threshold Setting | Allows users to configure acceptable impurity percentage |
| Scan Seaweed Button | Navigates to the seaweed scanning interface |

6. ## Seaweed Scanner Interface {#seaweed-scanner-interface}

The Seaweed Scanner interface allows users to capture images of harvested seaweed using the device camera. The captured images are processed by the AI model to determine seaweed quality indicators such as impurity percentage and discoloration.

![][image14]  
*Figure 3.6.1: Seaweed Scanner Interface*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Camera Preview Frame | Displays live camera feed |
| Capture Button | Allows manual capture of seaweed image |
| Quality Result HUD | Displays latest detection results |
| Home Button | Navigates back to the home screen |
| Auto Capture Toggle | Enables automatic scanning |
| Torch Toggle | Enables mobile phone flashlight |
| Recent Captures Button | Navigates to recent captures screen |

7. ## Recent Captures Screen {#recent-captures-screen}

This screen displays the most recent seaweed images captured by the scanner. Users can review captured images and verify scan results.

![][image15]  
*Figure 3.7.1: Recent Captures Screen*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Capture History List | Displays recent captured images |
| Scan Result Indicator | Shows quality result of each scan |
| Navigation Controls | Allows returning to scanner interface |

8. ## Recent Reports Screen {#recent-reports-screen}

The Recent Reports screen allows users to review previously recorded scan reports. This screen displays scan results along with their synchronization status.

![][image16]![][image17]  
*Figure 3.8.1: Recent Reports Screen (Pending), (Synced)*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Report Table | Displays recent scan results |
| Status Indicator | Shows whether scan data is Pending or Synced |
| Timestamp Column | Displays scan time |
| Quality Result Column | Displays impurity percentage and classification |

4. # Web Portal UI Screens {#web-portal-ui-screens}

   1. ## Landing Page {#landing-page}

The landing page serves as the entry point to the AquaLeaf website. It provides navigation options for farm account registration and login.

***![][image18]***  
*Figure 4.1.1: AquaLeaf Website Home Page*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Register Farm Account Button | Navigates to farm account registration page |
| Login Button | Navigates to user login page |
| System Information Section | Provides basic information about AquaLeaf |

2. ## Register Farm Account Page {#register-farm-account-page}

This page allows farm managers to register their seaweed farm accounts by submitting required registration details.

***![][image19]***  
*Figure 4.2.1: Register Farm Account Page*

![][image20]  
*Figure 4.2.2: Registration Process Loading Screen*

![][image21]  
*Figure 4.2.3: Registration Success Screen*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Farm Name Field | Input field for farm name |
| Location Field | Input field for farm location |
| Manager Name Field | Input field for manager name |
| Email Field | Input field for manager email |
| Password Field | Input field for account password |
| Register Button | Submits registration request |

3. ## Website Login Page {#website-login-page}

This page allows farm managers and administrators to authenticate into the AquaLeaf web portal.

![][image22]  
*Figure 4.3.1: Website Login Page*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Email or Farm Name Field | Input field for account identifier |
| Password Field | Input field for password |
| Login Button | Submits authentication request |

4. ## Report Generation Page {#report-generation-page}

The report generation page allows users to filter scan data and generate downloadable seaweed quality reports.

**![][image23]**  
*Figure 4.4.1: Report Filters and Data Preview Page*

**![][image24]**  
*Figure 4.4.2: Generated PDF Report*

**![][image25]**  
*Figure 4.4.3: Exported CSV Report*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Species Filter | Filters reports by seaweed species |
| Type Filter | Filters reports by seaweed type |
| Date Range Selector | Filters reports by date |
| Preview Table | Displays matching scan records |
| Generate PDF Button | Generates downloadable PDF report |
| Export CSV Button | Exports report data in CSV format |

5. ## Admin Dashboard – Usage Statistics {#admin-dashboard-–-usage-statistics}

The Usage Statistics section provides administrators with a summary of system activity. The statistics include the total number of registered farm accounts and aggregated seaweed scan data collected from the mobile application.

**![][image26]**  
*Figure 4.5.1: AquaLeaf Admin Dashboard – Usage Statistics Section*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Registered Farms Counter | Displays the total number of registered farm accounts |
| Total Scan Reports Counter | Displays the total number of seaweed scan records |
| Daily Scan Counter | Displays the number of scans submitted on the current day |
| Statistics Display Panel | Presents summarized system activity metrics |

6. ## Admin Dashboard – System Event Logs {#admin-dashboard-–-system-event-logs}

The System Event Logs section displays a chronological list of system activities and administrative actions recorded by the AquaLeaf system. These logs support auditing and traceability by providing visibility into administrator activities and farm account login events.

**![][image27]**  
*Figure 4.6.1: AquaLeaf Admin Dashboard – System Event Logs Section*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Event Log Table | Displays recorded system events |
| Event Type Column | Indicates type of recorded event |
| Actor Column | Displays account responsible for the event |
| Timestamp Column | Displays date and time of event |
| Filter Controls | Allows administrators to filter log entries |

7. ## Admin Dashboard – Manage Farm Accounts {#admin-dashboard-–-manage-farm-accounts}

The Manage Farm Accounts section allows administrators to view and manage registered farm accounts within the AquaLeaf system. Administrators can monitor account status and perform management actions such as suspending, reactivating, or deactivating accounts when required.

**![][image28]**  
*Figure 4.7.1: AquaLeaf Admin Dashboard – Manage Farm Accounts Section*

| Component | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| Farm Account Table | Displays list of registered farm accounts |
| Farm Name Column | Displays farm name |
| Email Column | Displays account email |
| Status Column | Displays current account status |
| Suspend Button | Temporarily disables farm account access |
| Reactivate Button | Restores access to suspended accounts |
| Deactivate Button | Permanently disables the farm account |
